channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Voters in Virginia could have one of the final words in Trump's redistricting fight
item.description: Virginians will decide whether the state will redraw its congressional voting map. A win would give Democrats an edge in four more seats, meaning they could hold 10 of Virginia's 11 seats in Congress.
item.content:encoded: Virginians will decide whether the state will redraw its congressional voting map. A win would give Democrats an edge in four more seats, meaning they could hold 10 of Virginia's 11 seats in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Jahd Khalil

item.title: Six months after ceasefire with Israel, people in Gaza say recovery hasn't even begun
item.description: Six months into a ceasefire that promised an end to the war and a surge of aid for Gaza, people say recovery hasn't even yet begun.
item.content:encoded: Six months into a ceasefire that promised an end to the war and a surge of aid for Gaza, people say recovery hasn't even yet begun.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: Dave Chappelle talks about comedy in the age of Trump
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin recently sat down with comedian Dave Chappelle for a conversation about comedy in the age of Trump, Chappelle's jokes about trans people, and much more.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin recently sat down with comedian Dave Chappelle for a conversation about comedy in the age of Trump, Chappelle's jokes about trans people, and much more.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: UN looks for a short-term solution for moving fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.N. looks for a quick solution to get cargo ships with fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz, to prevent a global food crisis.
item.content:encoded: The U.N. looks for a quick solution to get cargo ships with fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz, to prevent a global food crisis.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: This week in science: Small talk, more human lobster killing, and an ancient flood
item.description: NPR's Short Wave team talks about the surprising benefits of small talk, more humane ways to kill lobsters, and an ancient flood that may have helped create the Grand Canyon.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Short Wave team talks about the surprising benefits of small talk, more humane ways to kill lobsters, and an ancient flood that may have helped create the Grand Canyon.
item.dc:creator: Emily Kwong

item.title: Georgia jail uses drones to block dangerous deliveries to inmates
item.description: The Fulton County jail in Georgia is using drones to intercept deliveries of dangerous contraband to inmates. The drones are made by Flock, a company under scrutiny for its surveillance reach.
item.content:encoded: The Fulton County jail in Georgia is using drones to intercept deliveries of dangerous contraband to inmates. The drones are made by Flock, a company under scrutiny for its surveillance reach.
item.dc:creator: Chamian Cruz

item.title: Pope Leo vs. The Trump Administration
item.description: Pope Leo speaks out again about war and peace in a back and forth with President Trump, while the U.S. House speaker and vice president appear to question Leo's understanding of Catholic theology.
item.content:encoded: Pope Leo speaks out again about war and peace in a back and forth with President Trump, while the U.S. House speaker and vice president appear to question Leo's understanding of Catholic theology.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: California Attorney General Rob Bonta praises Live Nation verdict
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with California's Attorney General Rob Bonta about Wednesday's verdict in the Live Nation trial.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with California's Attorney General Rob Bonta about Wednesday's verdict in the Live Nation trial.
item.dc:creator: Justine Kenin

item.title: These musicians are providing the soundtrack for anti-ICE protests in LA
item.description: Los Jornaleros Del Norte play protest songs whose lyrics reflect the hopes and struggles of undocumented workers as they evade immigration agents patrolling the streets.
item.content:encoded:
Los Jornaleros Del Norte play protest songs whose lyrics reflect the hopes and struggles of undocumented workers as they evade immigration agents patrolling the streets.

(Image credit: Adrian Florido)
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: What's at stake in Somalia
item.description: Somalia's population is experiencing severe food insecurity after three years of drought. Now, the war in the Middle East means the import-dependent nation has even less access to food.
item.content:encoded: Somalia's population is experiencing severe food insecurity after three years of drought. Now, the war in the Middle East means the import-dependent nation has even less access to food.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis
